name: Industrial IoT
what: Networked sensors monitoring physical infrastructure.
how: Edge devices stream telemetry for analytics.

name: Smart Automation
what: Connected systems that self-optimise operations.
how: Closed-loop control adjusts to real-time data.

name: Smart City Platforms
what: City-scale sensing and coordination.
how: Data platforms optimise traffic, energy and services.

name: Digital Twin Cities
what: Real-time data from sensors and IoT devices to create a virtual model of the physical infrastructure.
how: Improving urban planning and resource management.

name: Precision Fermentation at City Scale
what: Using advanced biotechnology to produce high-value products in large-scale urban facilities.
how: Reducing costs and environmental impact compared to traditional manufacturing methods.

name: Quantum IoT
what: Quantum sensors for secure and efficient IoT communication.
how: Uses quantum entanglement to enhance network security.

name: Quantum Internet
use: Creating a global quantum communication network for secure data transfer.
what: Building a network of interconnected nodes using quantum entanglement for ultra-secure communications.
how: Deploying quantum repeaters and satellites to maintain signal integrity over long distances.

name: V2G Grid Stabilisation
what: Vehicle-to-Grid technology allowing EVs to push power back into the grid during peak demand.
how: Bidirectional chargers enable EV batteries to act as distributed energy resources (DERs).

name: Autonomous Drone Logistics Networks
what: Efficient transportation solutions for rural areas and disaster zones.
how: Using drones to deliver goods and services.

name: IoT City Infrastructure
what: Implementing IoT sensors and AI to optimize city infrastructure for sustainability and efficiency.
how: Deploying smart meters and predictive analytics for energy conservation and traffic flow optimization.

name: Global Digital Twin Cities
use: Urban infrastructure, resource management, citizen services
what: Digital twin cities integrate real-time data from sensors and IoT devices to optimize urban infrastructure, resource management, and citizen services.
how: This technology aims to create smarter, more sustainable cities that can adapt to changing needs.

name: City-Scale Digital Twins
what: Real-time 3D virtual replicas of physical cities.
how: Integrating IoT sensor arrays with BIM and GIS data for predictive simulation.

name: Autonomous Drone Logistics
what: Dynamic, AI-routed aerial networks for rapid transport of medical and commercial goods.
how: Implementing collision avoidance via LiDAR and federated swarm coordination.

name: V2G Grid Stabilization
what: Using electric vehicle batteries as distributed energy resources for the grid.
how: Bidirectional charging interfaces allowing the grid to pull power from EVs during peaks.

name: Smart Grid Optimization
what: AI-driven algorithms for real-time grid management and optimization.
how: Predictive analytics to prevent outages and improve efficiency.

name: Ambient Backscatter IoT
what: Battery-free sensors that communicate by reflecting existing radio signals rather than generating their own.
how: A tag modulates and reflects ambient TV, Wi-Fi or cellular signals to send data, harvesting the tiny power it needs from the same waves.

name: Terahertz 6G Sensing & Comms
what: Next-generation wireless that uses very high frequencies to carry more data and sense the environment at the same time.
how: Terahertz-band transceivers and reconfigurable surfaces are tested in labs for joint communication-and-sensing, shaping beams around obstacles.

name: Virtual Power Plants
what: Software that aggregates many home batteries, EVs and smart devices to act as one flexible grid resource.
how: A cloud controller forecasts demand and dispatches thousands of distributed assets in concert, trading their combined capacity into grid markets.

name: Direct-to-Device Satellite IoT
what: Low-Earth-orbit satellites that connect small sensors and phones directly, without ground infrastructure.
how: Constellations relay short low-power messages from unmodified or lightly modified devices, extending coverage to remote and ocean areas.
use: Remote asset tracking, agriculture and logistics sensing

name: Autonomous Delivery Robots
what: Small self-driving robots that carry goods along sidewalks and campuses for last-mile delivery.
how: Cameras, lidar and mapping let a wheeled robot navigate pedestrian paths, cross streets and hand off parcels, with remote operators on standby.
